data.title: Kraken receives EMI authorization from the UK Financial Conduct Authority
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, the cryptocurrency exchange Kraken announced that it has officially obtained the Electronic Money Institution (EMI) authorization issued by the UK Financial Conduct Authority (FCA), allowing it to issue electronic currency and speed up deposits and withdrawals for customers, thereby enhancing its market position in the UK.

data.title: BNB Drops Below 560 USDT with a 2.71% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Mar 10, 2025, 13:38 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has dropped below 560 USDT and is now trading at 559.900024 USDT, with a narrowed 2.71% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Bitfinex report: BTC enters a new fluctuation range of $85,000 to $92,000, and macroeconomic indicators do not indicate the market direction
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News After Bitcoin briefly recovered the $90,000 mark last week, it has now entered a new range of fluctuations between $85,000 and $92,000, and uncertainty continues to hang over the market. Prices soared early last week amid speculation about the U.S. strategic bitcoin reserve and the White House cryptocurrency summit, but as the news was confirmed, traders took a "sell the news" approach and the market quickly erased the gains. The options market also exacerbated last week's volatility, with $3 billion worth of Bitcoin and Ethereum contracts expiring last Friday, further driving price fluctuations. Many traders suffered heavy losses last week, with actual losses for market participants reaching $818 million per day. February 28 and March 4 were among the largest single-day losses in this cycle, and current macroeconomic indicators do not indicate a clear direction for future market development. 
Meanwhile, the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission's cryptocurrency working group will hold a roundtable on March 21 to clarify the security status of digital assets, suggesting that regulatory guidance will shift to a more structured approach. In Japan, the Liberal Democratic Party is implementing a tax reform that favors cryptocurrencies, reducing capital gains tax to 20% and classifying cryptocurrencies as a distinct asset class. The reform also proposes tax deferrals for swaps between cryptocurrencies and equal tax treatment for derivatives to encourage digital asset investment.

data.title: Michael Saylor Urges US to Buy 25% of Bitcoin Supply by 2035
data.description:
MicroStrategy co-founder Michael Saylor has proposed that the U.S. government secure 25% of the total Bitcoin supply over the next decade as part of the newly established Strategic Bitcoin Reserve. In Saylor’s proposal, “A Digital Assets Strategy to Dominate the 21st Century Global Economy”, the Bitcoin advocate outlined a plan for the U.S. government to amass 25% of the total Bitcoin supply...
source: https://news.shib.io/2025/03/10/michael-saylor-urges-us-to-buy-25-of-bitcoin-supply-by-2035/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=michael-saylor-urges-us-to-buy-25-of-bitcoin-supply-by-2035

data.title: UK Treasury says ‘no plans’ to introduce US-style bitcoin reserve
data.description:
Despite the US introducing a strategic bitcoin reserve, the UK still deems the currency too volatile and will not follow suit.
The post UK Treasury says ‘no plans’ to introduce US-style bitcoin reserve appeared first on Protos.
source: https://protos.com/uk-treasury-says-no-plans-to-introduce-us-style-bitcoin-reserve/

data.title: XRP Faces Significant Decline Amid Market Concerns
data.description:
According to CoinDesk, XRP, the cryptocurrency utilized by Ripple for cross-border transactions, experienced a sharp decline of over 27% in the week ending March 9. This marks its most significant weekly percentage drop since November 2022, as reported by TradingView and CoinDesk.

The recent sell-off has drawn attention to the crucial support level of $1.95. This level has historically served as a demand zone and is currently forming a head-and-shoulders (H&S) topping pattern, which has been developing since December. The H&S pattern is characterized by three peaks, with the middle peak being the highest, and a horizontal demand zone known as the neckline, identified by a trendline connecting the base of the three peaks.

A breach below the neckline indicates a weakening demand and a shift from a bullish to bearish market trend. This often results in deeper losses, equivalent to the gap between the neckline and the middle peak. Consequently, market bulls are tasked with defending the support near $2. Failure to do so could trigger the H&S breakdown, potentially leading to a decline to 60 cents, a level that posed significant resistance last year.

data.title: European Lawmakers Remain Silent On U.S. Bitcoin Reserve Order Amid Digital Euro Focus
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, European lawmakers have not publicly addressed the United States' recent decision to establish a Strategic Bitcoin Reserve, a significant policy shift that favors early Bitcoin adopters. U.S. President Donald Trump's executive order, issued on March 7, outlines a plan to create a Bitcoin reserve using cryptocurrency seized in criminal cases, rather than purchasing Bitcoin on the open market. This move has sparked curiosity about Europe's stance on integrating Bitcoin into national reserves, as European policymakers have yet to make any major public statements on the matter.

Anastasija Plotnikova, co-founder and CEO of blockchain regulatory firm Fideum, suggests that the silence may indicate a lack of European efforts to establish Bitcoin reserves due to the complex process of adding new assets to national treasuries. She notes that the European Central Bank (ECB) has historically been critical of Bitcoin as a reserve asset, effectively closing the door to its adoption by EU member states. Meanwhile, European lawmakers are focusing on the launch of the digital euro, a central bank digital currency (CBDC), which is expected to roll out in October 2025.

James Wo, founder and CEO of venture capital firm DFG, attributes the European silence on Trump's Bitcoin reserve order to the ECB's firm stance against holding Bitcoin in its reserves. ECB President Christine Lagarde has reiterated this position, emphasizing the EU's focus on the digital euro. However, recent issues with the ECB's Target 2 payment system, which caused significant transaction delays, have raised concerns about the ECB's ability to manage a digital currency.

Despite skepticism, Lagarde is pushing forward with the digital euro's rollout, emphasizing that it will coexist with cash and offer privacy protections to address concerns about government overreach. The European Union's approach contrasts with the United States, where President Trump has taken a firm stance against CBDCs. While CBDCs are praised for their potential to enhance financial inclusion, critics warn of their surveillance capabilities and potential for government overreach. In a related development, Brazil's central bank published the source code for its CBDC pilot in July 2023, revealing surveillance and control mechanisms that allow the central bank to freeze or reduce user funds within CBDC wallets.

data.title: Rex Shares And Osprey Funds Plan To Launch ETF Tracking MOVE Performance
data.description:
According to PANews, investment management firms Rex Shares and Osprey Funds have submitted an application to list an ETF that tracks the price performance of MOVE, a product of Movement Networks. The mainnet of this Ethereum Layer 2 network, built using MoveVM, is expected to launch later on Monday.

The application filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) indicates that the proposed REX-Osprey MOVE ETF will allocate at least 80% of its assets to MOVE or related instruments, through direct holdings and derivatives.

Rex Shares, known for issuing ETFs, has previously collaborated with Osprey Funds to submit applications for listing funds, including products related to meme coins such as TRUMP, BONK, and DOGE.

data.title: Japan Reduces Bitcoin Tax Rate Amid Cryptocurrency Reforms
data.description: According to Foresight News, Japan has reduced the tax rate on Bitcoin earnings from 55% to 20%. This change is part of a broader reform of the country's cryptocurrency regulations.
